production.speaker-map: Alex: alice.wav Blair: chandra.wav Casey: david.wav
production.script: Alex: I keep telling myself this corridor is ordinary, but the walls lean in every time I breathe. Blair: Then stop narrating and open the door. Alex: If I open it, the thought becomes a fact. Blair: If you do not open it, the fear stays in charge.

Casey: The city below them looked arranged, almost edited, every light in its assigned column of rain. Alex: You make it sound like the storm filed a report before arriving. Casey: It did, and the report said we were already late. Alex: Then read me the rest before the rooftop gives up.

Blair: The trail bends by the river, so if they are ahead of us we should hear them before we see them. Casey: I hear water, wind, and your backpack complaining. Blair: The backpack is making a legal objection. Casey: Good. Let it object quietly while we cross.

Alex: The market is three blocks away and somehow every vendor is already arguing with the weather. Blair: That is how you know it is a real market. Alex: If the rain gets louder, we may have to shout prices back at them. Blair: Practice on me first. I only overpay under pressure.

Casey: This kitchen always sounds fuller after midnight, like the tile remembers every conversation. Alex: That explains why the room keeps repeating your bad ideas. Casey: The room has excellent taste. Alex: The room also needs tea before I accept its judgment.

Blair: Close the office door and listen to that echo. Even the filing cabinets sound impatient. Casey: They have heard this budget meeting four times already. Blair: Then let us disappoint them quickly and go home. Casey: Fine, but the stapler stays. It is the only one on our side.
